title: arthraxon ciliaris subsp. langsdorffii
intro: Arthraxon ciliaris subsp. langsdorffii, also known as Langsdorff's bent grass or Langsdorff's bent, is a perennial grass species native to China and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Arthraxon ciliaris subsp. langsdorffii is often dried and used as an ingredient in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
